messages.content:
How is the hydrogen in heavy water different from the hydrogen in normal water?





The hydrogen in heavy water is radioactive.



The hydrogen atoms in heavy water have one neutron in the nucleus, doubling the mass.





Hydrogen atoms in heavy water have an extra proton in the nucleus.

Heavy water molecules contain 3 hydrogen atoms instead of 2.
The hydrogen atoms in heavy water have one neutron in the nucleus, doubling the mass.

What does the number under the symbol for each element represent?



The average mass of










mean atomic





The average number of isotopes





The average atomic mass
The average atomic mass.

How is the name of each element on the periodic table represented?





Letters are assigned to each element by the scientist who discovered it.





Letters are given to represent each item based on when it was discovered.



One or two letters are used to represent the name of each element.





The first two letters of the element name are used for the table.
One or two letters are used to represent the name of each element.
Is there anything else I can help you with?

Question 4 of 10

What is an ion? A. An atom that has lost or gained 1 or more neutrons B. An atom that differs in mass from another atom of the same element C. An atom that has lost or gained 1 or more
An ion is an atom that has lost or gained one or more electrons.

Sodium (Na) has 11 protons and 12 neutrons. What is its isotopic notation?



TO.

Na-11



b.

Na-12



c.

Na-23



d.

Na-34
b. Na-12

Chlorine (Cl) has an atomic number of 17. It often forms an ion by gaining 1 electron. What would your charge be?





+1





-1








16



18
The charge on the chlorine (Cl) ion formed by gaining an electron would be -1.

What is the periodic table?





A list of major achievements in science





A calendar of advances in chemistry





A table of when the elements were discovered





An arrangement of known elements
A periodic table is an arrangement of known elements in tabular form based on their chemical and physical properties.

An aluminum (Al) atom has an atomic number of 13 and a mass number of 27. How many neutrons does it have?





13





14





27





40
An aluminum (Al) atom has a mass number of 27 and an atomic number of 13. Therefore, it has 14 neutrons (mass number - atomic number = number of neutrons).

99.9% of the matter that makes up living organisms consists of how many elements?





6





25




90







118
99.9% of the matter that makes up living organisms consists of 6 elements.

Potassium (K) has an atomic number of 19. It often forms an ion by losing 1 electron. What charge would the ion have?





+1





-1





18





twenty
The charge on the potassium (K) ion formed by losing an electron would be +1.
